From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/76/Time_zones_of_Brazil.svg/500px-Time_zones_of_Brazil.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Time_zones_of_Brazil.svg)

Time in Brazil, since 25 April 2019.

**Time in Brazil**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | ACT | [Acre Time](/wiki/Acre_Time "Acre Time") | [UTC−5](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9205:00 "UTC−05:00") | (BRT–2) |
|  | AMT | [Amazon Time](/wiki/Amazon_Time "Amazon Time") | [UTC−4](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9204:00 "UTC−04:00") | (BRT−1) |
|  | BRT | [Brasília Time](/wiki/Bras%C3%ADlia_Time "Brasília Time") | [UTC−3](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00") | (BRT) |
|  | FNT | [Fernando de Noronha Time](/wiki/Fernando_de_Noronha_Time "Fernando de Noronha Time") | [UTC−2](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9202:00 "UTC−02:00") | (BRT+1) |

**Time in Brazil** is calculated using [standard time](/wiki/Standard_time "Standard time"), and the country (including its offshore islands) is divided into four standard [time zones](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone"): **Fernando de Noronha time** (UTC−02:00), **Brasília time** (UTC−03:00), **Amazon time** (UTC−04:00), and **Acre time** (UTC−05:00).[[1]](#cite_note-1) About 93% of the Brazilian population live in Brasília time (UTC−03:00).[[2]](#cite_note-census-2)

## Time zones

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Time zones")]

### Fernando de Noronha time (UTC−02:00)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Fernando de Noronha time (UTC−02:00)")]

This is the standard time zone only on a few small offshore [Atlantic](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean") islands. The only such island with a permanent population is [Fernando de Noronha](/wiki/Fernando_de_Noronha "Fernando de Noronha"), with 3,167 inhabitants (2022 census), 0.0016% of [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil")'s population.[[2]](#cite_note-census-2) The other islands ([Trindade and Martim Vaz](/wiki/Trindade_and_Martim_Vaz "Trindade and Martim Vaz"), [Rocas Atoll](/wiki/Rocas_Atoll "Rocas Atoll") and [Saint Peter and Saint Paul Archipelago](/wiki/Saint_Peter_and_Saint_Paul_Archipelago "Saint Peter and Saint Paul Archipelago")) either are totally uninhabited or have small seasonally rotating [Brazilian Navy](/wiki/Brazilian_Navy "Brazilian Navy") garrisons or teams of scientists.

### Brasília time (UTC−03:00)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Brasília time (UTC−03:00)")]

The main time zone of Brazil comprises the states in the [South](/wiki/South_Region,_Brazil "South Region, Brazil"), [Southeast](/wiki/Southeast_Region,_Brazil "Southeast Region, Brazil") and [Northeast](/wiki/Northeast_Region,_Brazil "Northeast Region, Brazil") regions (except the small islands mentioned above), plus the states of [Goiás](/wiki/Goi%C3%A1s "Goiás"), [Tocantins](/wiki/Tocantins "Tocantins"), [Pará](/wiki/Par%C3%A1 "Pará") and [Amapá](/wiki/Amap%C3%A1 "Amapá"), and the [Federal District](/wiki/Federal_District_(Brazil) "Federal District (Brazil)"), which includes the national capital city, [Brasília](/wiki/Bras%C3%ADlia "Brasília"). About 93% of the Brazilian population live in this time zone, which covers about 60% of the country's land area.[[2]](#cite_note-census-2)

### Amazon time (UTC−04:00)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Amazon time (UTC−04:00)")]

This time zone is used in the states of [Mato Grosso](/wiki/Mato_Grosso "Mato Grosso"), [Mato Grosso do Sul](/wiki/Mato_Grosso_do_Sul "Mato Grosso do Sul"), [Rondônia](/wiki/Rond%C3%B4nia "Rondônia"), [Roraima](/wiki/Roraima "Roraima"), and most of [Amazonas](/wiki/Amazonas_(Brazilian_state) "Amazonas (Brazilian state)"). Although this time zone covers about 36% of the land area of Brazil (an area larger than [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina "Argentina")), only about 6% of the country's population live there (about 12 million people, slightly more than the city of [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo")).[[2]](#cite_note-census-2)

Until 2008, the part of the state of [Pará](/wiki/Par%C3%A1 "Pará") west of the [Jari](/wiki/Jari_River "Jari River") and [Xingu](/wiki/Xingu_River "Xingu River") rivers was also part of this time zone; then it joined the rest of the state in observing Brasília time (UTC−03:00). Although other changes to Brazilian time zones enacted at that time have since been reverted (see below), western Pará still remains in UTC−03:00.

### Acre time (UTC−05:00)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Acre time (UTC−05:00)")]

This time zone was reinstated in late 2013, after having been abolished for over five years. It is used in the far-western tip of the country, which includes the entire state of [Acre](/wiki/Acre_(state) "Acre (state)") and the southwestern portion of the state of [Amazonas](/wiki/Amazonas_(Brazilian_state) "Amazonas (Brazilian state)") (west of a line connecting the cities of [Tabatinga](/wiki/Tabatinga "Tabatinga") and [Porto Acre](/wiki/Porto_Acre "Porto Acre")).[[a]](#cite_note-amazonas-7) These areas cover only about 4% of the Brazilian territory (although that is still about the size of [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany")) and have only about 0.5% of the country's population (little more than one million people).[[2]](#cite_note-census-2)

On 24 June 2008, these areas advanced their clocks by an hour, so that they became part of the UTC−04:00 time zone.[[7]](#cite_note-8) However, in a [non-binding referendum](/wiki/2010_Acre_time_zone_referendum "2010 Acre time zone referendum") held on 31 October 2010, a slight majority of Acre voters voted in favour of returning the state to UTC−05:00.[[8]](#cite_note-9) On 30 October 2013, [Brazilian President](/wiki/President_of_Brazil "President of Brazil") [Dilma Rousseff](/wiki/Dilma_Rousseff "Dilma Rousseff") enacted Law 12876, establishing that the time zone switch would occur on Sunday, 10 November 2013.[[9]](#cite_note-10) Since then, the state of Acre and the southwestern part of the state of Amazonas[[a]](#cite_note-amazonas-7) are again 5 hours behind UTC.

### Unofficial observance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Unofficial observance")]

Unofficially, 32 municipalities in eastern [Mato Grosso](/wiki/Mato_Grosso "Mato Grosso"),[[b]](#cite_note-11) located in the [Araguaia](/wiki/Araguaia_River "Araguaia River") valley, observe UTC−03:00, Brasília time.[[10]](#cite_note-12)[[11]](#cite_note-13)[[12]](#cite_note-14)

Unofficially, some municipalities in eastern [Mato Grosso do Sul](/wiki/Mato_Grosso_do_Sul "Mato Grosso do Sul"), such as [Bataguassu](/wiki/Bataguassu "Bataguassu"), [Aparecida do Taboado](/wiki/Aparecida_do_Taboado "Aparecida do Taboado"), [Cassilândia](/wiki/Cassil%C3%A2ndia "Cassilândia") and [Chapadão do Sul](/wiki/Chapad%C3%A3o_do_Sul "Chapadão do Sul"), also observe UTC−03:00, Brasília time.[[13]](#cite_note-15)[[14]](#cite_note-16)

## Daylight saving time

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Daylight saving time")]

Main article: [Daylight saving time in Brazil](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time_in_Brazil "Daylight saving time in Brazil")

Brazil observed [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") (DST; [Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"): *horário de verão*, "summer time") in the years of 1931–1933, 1949–1953, 1963–1968 and 1985–2019. Initially it applied to the whole country, but from 1988 it applied only to part of the country, usually the southern regions, where DST is more useful due to a larger seasonal variation in daylight duration. It typically lasted from October or November to February or March.[[15]](#cite_note-decrees-17)

The most recent DST rule specified advancing the time by one hour during the period from 00:00 on the first Sunday in November to 00:00 on the third Sunday in February (postponed by one week if the latter fell on [carnival](/wiki/Brazilian_Carnival "Brazilian Carnival")), applicable only to the [South](/wiki/South_Region,_Brazil "South Region, Brazil"), [Southeast](/wiki/Southeast_Region,_Brazil "Southeast Region, Brazil") and [Central-West](/wiki/Central-West_Region,_Brazil "Central-West Region, Brazil") regions,[[15]](#cite_note-decrees-17) which comprise about 65% of the Brazilian population.[[2]](#cite_note-census-2) During DST, Brasília time moved from UTC−03:00 to UTC−02:00; the other states that did not follow DST observed a change of the offset to Brasília time.

Brazil abolished DST in 2019.[[15]](#cite_note-decrees-17)

## IANA time zone database

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: IANA time zone database")]

The [IANA time zone database](/wiki/IANA_time_zone_database "IANA time zone database") contains 16 zones for Brazil. Columns marked with \* are from the file [zone.tab](/wiki/Zone.tab "Zone.tab") of the database.

| c.c.\* | coordinates\* | TZ\* | comments\* | UTC offset | DST |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | −0351−03225 | America/Noronha | Atlantic islands | [−02:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9202:00 "UTC−02:00") | —N/a |
| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | −0127−04829 | America/Belem | Pará (east), Amapá | [−03:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00") | —N/a |
| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | −0343−03830 | America/Fortaleza | Brazil (northeast: MA, PI, CE, RN, PB) | [−03:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00") | —N/a |
| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | −0803−03454 | America/Recife | Pernambuco | [−03:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00") | —N/a |
| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | −0712−04812 | America/Araguaina | Tocantins | [−03:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00") | —N/a |
| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | −0940−03543 | America/Maceio | Alagoas, Sergipe | [−03:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00") | —N/a |
| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | −1259−03831 | America/Bahia | Bahia | [−03:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00") | —N/a |
| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | −2332−04637 | America/Sao\_Paulo | Brazil (southeast: GO, DF, MG, ES, RJ, SP, PR, SC, RS) | [−03:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9203:00 "UTC−03:00") | —N/a |
| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | −2027−05437 | America/Campo\_Grande | Mato Grosso do Sul | [−04:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9204:00 "UTC−04:00") | —N/a |

| [BR](/wiki/ISO_3166-1:BR "ISO 3166-1:BR") | +0249−06040 | America/Boa\_Vista | Roraima | [−04:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9204:00 "UTC−04:00") | —N/a |

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Time_in_Brazil&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-amazonas_7-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-amazonas_7-1) The municipalities of [Atalaia do Norte](/wiki/Atalaia_do_Norte "Atalaia do Norte"), [Eirunepé](/wiki/Eirunep%C3%A9 "Eirunepé"), [Envira](/wiki/Envira "Envira"), [Guajará](/wiki/Guajar%C3%A1 "Guajará") and [Ipixuna](/wiki/Ipixuna "Ipixuna") are located entirely west of the line. Part of the municipalities of [Benjamin Constant](/wiki/Benjamin_Constant,_Amazonas "Benjamin Constant, Amazonas") and [Tabatinga](/wiki/Tabatinga "Tabatinga"), including their seats and most of their population, are also located west of the line. Part of the municipalities of [Boca do Acre](/wiki/Boca_do_Acre "Boca do Acre"), [Itamarati](/wiki/Itamarati "Itamarati"), [Jutaí](/wiki/Juta%C3%AD "Jutaí"), [Pauini](/wiki/Pauini "Pauini") and [São Paulo de Olivença](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo_de_Oliven%C3%A7a "São Paulo de Olivença") are located west of the line, but their seats and almost their entire population are located east of it.[[3]](#cite_note-3) [Amaturá](/wiki/Amatur%C3%A1 "Amaturá") and [Lábrea](/wiki/L%C3%A1brea "Lábrea") are sometimes cited as municipalities in UTC−05:00,[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) but they are located entirely east of the line. The seat of Lábrea uses UTC−04:00.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
